title: Asia Centric: How AI and Energy Security Fuel a New Supercycle
summary: A massive convergence of artificial intelligence, energy and security is sparking what is likely to be the largest and longest energy investment cycle in history, according to Morgan Stanley. Driven by the demands of AI data centers and a strategic shift from “just-in-time” cost efficiency to “just-in-case” resilience, more than $5 trillion in new-energy investments is projected for Asia over the next five years.

title: Rheinmetall Wins German Laser Deal After Frigate Fiasco
summary: German lawmakers approved more than a dozen military procurement deals worth over €12 billion ($13.7 billion), including a laser weapon contract with defense giant Rheinmetall AG and a warship order for domestic rival TKMS AG & Co. KGaA.

title: Campaigning Democrats Amplify Calls to Overhaul Supreme Court
summary: Democratic candidates are promising to overhaul the Supreme Court and embracing bolder positions in midterm campaigning than the lawmakers they’d replace—a sign of how angry the left is about the direction of the conservative-dominated court.

title: Oil Extends Surge as Fresh US Strikes Against Iran Rattle Market
summary: Oil extended a powerful surge as the US struck targets in Iran for a second day, raising risks for energy supplies from the region.

title: Samsung, SK Hynix and Leveraged ETFs Drive 70% of Korea Trading
summary: Retail fervor over leveraged ETFs in South Korea has grown so intense that the products, together with the two chipmaker stocks they track, now make up more than 70% of trading value in the $4.3 trillion market.

title: Noteholders to Aston Martin Sign Cooperation Pact Amid Debt Rout
summary: A group of creditors to luxury car maker Aston Martin has signed a cooperation pact as earnings pressure weighs on the company’s debt.

title: Harley-Davidson’s Credit Ratings Get Cut to Junk By S&P
summary: S&P Global Ratings cut Harley-Davidson Inc.’s credit grades to junk status on Wednesday, citing the company’s strategy to start selling lower-cost motorcycles to boost revenue.

title: Oil Gains, US Stock Futures Drop on New Strikes: Markets Wrap
summary: Oil gained and US equity futures ticked lower after the US said it is striking Iran for the second straight day, an escalation of violence that threatens to choke shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Fmr. HUD Secretary on Toyota’s Texas Shift, Mexico Trade
summary: Henry Cisneros, former US Department of Housing and Urban Development Secretary and former Mayor of San Antonio, discussed the origins and significance of Toyota's investment in San Antonio. He recounted how Toyota executives chose San Antonio for a new plant after observing the high number of pickup trucks at a Dallas Cowboys game, indicating strong local demand. He speaks with Romaine Bostick & Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Trump Says Ukraine Can Make Patriot Missiles
summary: American Enterprise Institute Senior Fellow Angela Stent  discusses President Trump's announcement he would allow Ukraine to make its own Patriot Missiles. She says Trump "sees that Ukraine looks like a winner, that it has cards that the Ukrainians are really fighting back." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Soars as US Extends Strikes Against Iran Into Second Night
summary: Oil jumped to a two-week high amid renewed US attacks on Iran and President Donald Trump’s assessment that the interim peace deal is a dead letter, heightening concerns about the free flow of crude from a key energy-producing region.

title: Trader Joe's, Lowe's Embrace Mini Mania To Boost Sales
summary: Mini mania is sweeping through US retailers as shoppers on a budget love colorful, charming, tiny versions of common products. Bloomberg’s Sunny Kim has more.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: GrainCorp Pushes for Australia Biofuel Mandates After Iran War
summary: Australia should adopt national biofuel mandates to bolster its energy security, according to GrainCorp Ltd., after months of uncertainty from the Iran war which sent shockwaves through the country’s economy.

title: Emerging Currencies Slide as US Forces Launch New Iran Strikes
summary: Most emerging-market currencies weakened on Wednesday as the US launched a new wave of strikes against Iran after threatening to reimpose an oil embargo, renewing fears of a prolonged conflict and bolstering oil prices.

title: Media M&A in Focus at Sun Valley
summary:
Bloomberg's Michelle Davis joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals" to discuss what's top of mind in media M&A as moguls gather in Sun Valley for Allen & Co.'s annual conference.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Fall as US-Iran Jitters Spur Rally in Oil: Markets Wrap
summary: A flare-up in geopolitical risks sent stocks and bonds lower as oil jumped after President Donald Trump said a ceasefire with Iran may be over, raising concerns about a potential return to all-out war.

title: US Stocks Pare Iran War-Driven Losses After Dip Buyers Emerge
summary: US equities nearly erased early losses that were driven by fears of US-Iran war escalation that pushed energy prices higher.

title: Economic Costs For US, Iran Too Great for 'All-Out War,' Says CSIS's Will Todman
summary: Will Todman, chief of staff of the Geopolitics and Foreign Policy Department and senior fellow of the Middle East Program at CSIS, said that a low level conflict with outbursts from both the US and Iran is more likely than an 'all-out' war because the economic costs to both countries are too great. Todman said that Iran is 'very keen' to keep control over the Strait of Hormuz as an economic and strategic negotiating tool.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Jumps to Two Week High on US-Iran Jitters | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Bailey Lipschultz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hyperscaler Debt Is Now a Rates Story
summary: When so much supply hits the market.

title: SpaceX Has 'Tremendous' Earnings Power, Say Clear Street Analysts
summary: Brian Dobson, managing director & senior analyst, and Greg Pendy, director & equity analyst, at Clear Street discuss their buy rating on SpaceX and say that they're looking at the rocket business as a 'placeholder' for other growing parts of the business like Starlink or xAI.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Accor to Tap Banks for Hoxton Owner Ennismore’s US IPO
summary: Accor SA has lined up investment banks to arrange a potential US initial public offering of Ennismore Lifestyle Group Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter, as the French hotel group presses on with plans to spin out the unit.

title: Long-Term Linear Valuation Not Promising: Lee
summary: Grace Lee, partner & managing director and co-lead of the media & entertainment practice at Alix Partners, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." They discuss media dealmaking as executives descended this week on Sun Valley, Idaho, for the Allen & Co. annual gathering of media, technology and finance moguls.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Treasury Two-Year Yield Near 2026 High in Global Bond Selloff
summary: The two-year US Treasury yield climbed back toward last month’s peak amid a selloff in global bond markets as rising oil prices rekindled worries about inflation.

title: Torrid Pace for Deals: Paul Weiss's Veeraraghavan
summary:
Krishna Veeraraghavan, global co-head of M&A at Paul Weiss, joins Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Deals." They discuss the current pace of M&A deals, capital coming back into public markets, and AI continuing to drive deal activity. 
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Los Angeles Hospital Blames Covid, Lender Dispute for Bankruptcy
summary: Pacifica Hospital of the Valley, which serves poor and homeless residents in Los Angeles, filed for bankruptcy, blaming old financial problems linked to the Covid-19 pandemic and a more recent dispute with lenders.

title: Zillow, Rocket to Face August FTC Trial Over Rental Listings
summary: Zillow Group Inc. and Rocket Companies Inc.’s Redfin are headed to trial next month in the US Federal Trade Commission’s challenge to an apartment-listing partnership, after a judge in Virginia declined a government request to decide the case early.

title: Gold Drops on Rate-Hike Fears as Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over
summary: Gold dropped as US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran has ended, stoking concerns that a renewal of fighting could again drive inflation and push up interest rates.

title: Strait of Hormuz Is Iran's 'New Nuclear Weapon' Says Miller
summary: Aaron David Miller, senior fellow at Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, joined Balance of Power to discuss the recent escalation in the Middle East. He said Iran will go to "extreme lengths" to ensure it exercises some control over the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China Adds to US Soy Buying With Largest Daily Deal Since 2025
summary: China purchased the largest amount of US soybeans since November, extending a wave of buying as agricultural trade between the world’s two largest economies gathers pace.
